rulings.Case Title: ERIC D PARKE VS. KETAM BSISSO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Augment Expert Witness List
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 5, 2010, line 15, DEFENDANTS KETAM BSISSO, SAMI BSISSO, AND ROBERT SHREVE'S Motion To Augment Expert Witness List. DENIED. NEUROPSYCHOLOGICAL TESTING WAS REFERENCED THROUGHOUT THE COURSE OF THE CASE. THE MOTION IS NOT BROUGHT WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. REASONABLE SANCTIONS AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,300 (CCP SECTION 2034.630) (11 HOURS X REASONABLE BILLING RATE OF $300/HOUR.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC DBA GREAT WESTERN VS. DAVID BASS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 5, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANTS DAVID BASS AND MIHUI BASS Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. DENIED. THE COURT DOES NOT RULE ON THE LEGALITY OF THE INTEREST RATE. HOWEVER, EVEN AN IMPROPER INTEREST REQUEST WOULD BE SUBJECT TO SEVERANCE. A PARTY CAN BRING AN UNJUST ENRICHMENT CAUSE OF ACTION WHERE A CONTRACT IS ILLEGAL. SEE DUNKIN V. BOSKEY (2000) 82 CAL.APP.4TH 171. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JACOB BRISKMAN et al VS. TAGGED, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltfs Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 5, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS TAGGED, INC., GREG TSENG, JOHANN SCHLEIER-SMITH, LOUIS WILLACY, DEVIN DWORAK, DIEGO MATUTE, AND GERRY TANO'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint. MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO CONSPIRACY FOR PLAINTIFFS TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS TOOK ILLEGAL ACTIONS OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF THEIR EMPLOYMENT IN AN EFFORT FOR PERSONAL GAIN. PLAINTIFFS MUST SEEK COMPENSATORY DAMAGES UNDERLYING THEIR PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIMS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JACOB BRISKMAN et al VS. TAGGED, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 5, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS TAGGED, INC., GREG TSENG, JOHANN SCHLEIER-SMITH, LOUIS WILLACY, DEVIN DWORAK, DIEGO MATUTE, AND GERRY TANO'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD THE BENEFITS EACH PLAINTIFF CONFERRED ON THE DEFENDANTS WHO WERE THEN UNJUSTLY ENRICHED AT PLAINTIFFS EXPENSE. AS TO DEFENDANTS SCHLEIR-SMITH AND DWORAK, PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD FACTS SUPPORTING KNOWLEDGE OR AUTHORIZATION OF ANY ILLEGAL ACTS. PLAINTIFFS TO INCLUDE NEGLIGENCE PER SE ALLEGATIONS IN THEIR NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO DEFENDANTS TSENG, WILLACY, MATUTE, AND TANO. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MINOTT WESSINGER AS TRUSTEE OF THE MINOTT VS. EDWARD KAVOUNAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re-Notice) Of Deft'S Motion And Motion To Copel Compliance With Court Order And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 5, 2010, line 3, EDMOND KAVOUNAS AND MARGARET KAVOUNAS (Re-Notice) Of Defendant's Motion To CoMpel Compliance With Court Order And For Sanctions. GRANTED. PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE ACCESS BY 10/15/10 AND REMIT $1,500 IN SANCTIONS TO THE COURT. THE COURT ORDERED PLAINTIFF TO ALLOW WEATHERPROOFING. PLAINTIFF'S ATTEMPT TO INJECT CONDITIONS IN THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS THAT WERE UNRELATED TO THE WEATHERPROOFING AND SUBSEQUENT REFUSAL TO PROVIDE ACCESS WAS A VIOLATION OF THIS COURT'S ORDER TO MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. ELIZABETH LEE, et al.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 5, 2010, line 13, DEFENDANT ELIZABETH LEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE 3-DAY NOTICE AND LEASE AS MATTERS NOT REASONABLY SUBJECT TO DISPUTE. SEE EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 452(h); ASCHERMAN V. GENERAL REINSURANCE GROUP (1986) 183 CAL.APP.3D 307, 310. THE COURT SUSTAINS WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE THE 3-DAY NOTICE IS DEFECTIVE AS A MATTER OF LAW. THE NOTICE DOES NOT SPECIFY THE COVENANT BREACHED NOR THE BASIS FOR EVICTION UNDER THE SAN FRANCISCO ADMINISTRATIVE CODE. =(302/CWW)
